steps.title: Find the supplement
steps.content:
The angle marked $130^\circ$ is exterior to the triangle formed by the two transversals and the parallel lines. The adjacent interior angle is
$$
180^\circ-130^\circ=50^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the exterior angle
steps.content:
By the parallel-line angle relationships, one remote interior angle is $50^\circ+b^\circ$ and the other is $b^\circ$. The exterior angle equals the sum of those remote interior angles:
$$
130^\circ=(50^\circ+b^\circ)+b^\circ
$$

steps.title: Solve for the angle
steps.content: Solve the equation: $$ 130^\circ=50^\circ+2b^\circ $$ $$ 80^\circ=2b^\circ $$ $$ 40^\circ=b^\circ $$ The answer is A.
